Chamaecrista niqueroensis is further classified within the genus Chamaecrista, which includes approximately 150 species of plants. intro: Chamaecrista niqueroensis, also known as Niquero partridge pea, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Chamaecrista niqueroensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Chamaecrista niqueroensis is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to treat various health conditions.
